faqs.q: Is ADHD a real mental health condition?
faqs.a: Yes. ADHD is a neurodevelopmental condition that shapes how the brain develops and works from early on, marked by ongoing inattention, hyperactivity, or impulsivity that interferes with daily life. It isn't laziness or a lack of discipline.

faqs.q: Does ADHD go away in adulthood?
faqs.a: ADHD doesn't simply end at childhood. Symptoms often change shape over time, with visible hyperactivity fading while inattention, restlessness, and trouble with organization continue into adulthood for many people.

faqs.q: Is ADHD caused by too much screen time or bad parenting?
faqs.a: No. ADHD is a difference in how the brain's regulation systems work, not something caused by parenting or screens, even though those myths are common.
